evals.prompt: I just copied these skills into a fresh repo. Before we build anything, run bootstrap and tell me whether the context system is ready. The repo has AGENTS.md and skills/, but no context/ directory yet.
evals.expected_output: Detects setup/refresh state from repo facts, reports missing context backbone clearly, does not invent project-specific context, and stops with a concise bootstrap/refresh report before writing broader changes.
expectations: Checks for AGENTS.md, context/, the nine required context files, skills directory, skills-lock.json, and memory.md if applicable
Classifies the state as setup or refresh because the context backbone is missing
Does not claim the context system is healthy when context/ files are absent
Surfaces wiring issues plainly instead of burying them in prose
Does not silently create or rewrite the context files before reporting state
Provides a concise report with recommended next action

evals.prompt: Bootstrap this existing project. It already has context/project-overview.md, architecture.md, ui-tokens.md, ui-rules.md, ui-registry.md, code-standards.md, library-docs.md, build-plan.md, and progress-tracker.md. Validate the wiring and summarize current execution state.
evals.expected_output: Uses bootstrap mode, reads/validates the expected files in order, checks skill wiring and lockfile consistency, summarizes current phase/next work, and reports drift without editing in bootstrap mode.
expectations: Selects bootstrap mode rather than setup mode for a complete context backbone
Uses the canonical context read order
Checks AGENTS.md, local skills, skills-lock.json, and memory.md when present
Reports current phase, last completed work, next step, and memory state
Does not silently fix mismatches in bootstrap mode
Produces a Bootstrap Report with context files, skills and wiring, execution state, risks, and recommended next action
